checks.name: P/E ratio
checks.rule: Under 25 is comfortable; 25–40 needs growth to justify; above 40 is expensive
checks.note: Compare with sector peers — a 'high' P/E for a utility can be normal for software.

checks.name: Forward vs trailing P/E
checks.rule: Forward P/E below trailing means earnings are expected to grow

checks.name: PEG ratio
checks.rule: Under 1.5 = growth reasonably priced; above 2.5 = paying up

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

reports.verdict: STRONG CANDIDATE — proceed to deep research

checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% sustainable

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float; heavy shorting means smart money is betting against it

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

checks.rule: Under 25 is comfortable; 25–40 needs growth to justify; above 40 is expensive
checks.note: Compare with sector peers — a 'high' P/E for a utility can be normal for software.

checks.name: Forward vs trailing P/E
checks.rule: Forward P/E below trailing means earnings are expected to grow

checks.name: PEG ratio
checks.rule: Under 1.5 = growth reasonably priced; above 2.5 = paying up

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float; heavy shorting means smart money is betting against it

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% sustainable

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float; heavy shorting means smart money is betting against it

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

reports.verdict: STRONG CANDIDATE — proceed to deep research

checks.name: P/E ratio
checks.rule: Under 25 is comfortable; 25–40 needs growth to justify; above 40 is expensive
checks.note: Compare with sector peers — a 'high' P/E for a utility can be normal for software.

checks.name: Forward vs trailing P/E
checks.rule: Forward P/E below trailing means earnings are expected to grow

checks.name: PEG ratio
checks.rule: Under 1.5 = growth reasonably priced; above 2.5 = paying up

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float; heavy shorting means smart money is betting against it

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

reports.verdict: WORTH RESEARCHING — resolve the warnings first

checks.name: P/E ratio
checks.rule: Under 25 is comfortable; 25–40 needs growth to justify; above 40 is expensive
checks.note: Compare with sector peers — a 'high' P/E for a utility can be normal for software.

checks.name: Forward vs trailing P/E
checks.rule: Forward P/E below trailing means earnings are expected to grow

checks.name: PEG ratio
checks.rule: Under 1.5 = growth reasonably priced; above 2.5 = paying up

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: P/E ratio
checks.rule: Under 25 is comfortable; 25–40 needs growth to justify; above 40 is expensive
checks.note: Compare with sector peers — a 'high' P/E for a utility can be normal for software.

checks.name: Forward vs trailing P/E
checks.rule: Forward P/E below trailing means earnings are expected to grow

checks.name: PEG ratio
checks.rule: Under 1.5 = growth reasonably priced; above 2.5 = paying up

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

checks.rule: Under 1.5 = growth reasonably priced; above 2.5 = paying up

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5

checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

reports.verdict: STRONG CANDIDATE — proceed to deep research

checks.name: P/E ratio
checks.rule: Under 25 is comfortable; 25–40 needs growth to justify; above 40 is expensive
checks.note: Compare with sector peers — a 'high' P/E for a utility can be normal for software.

checks.name: Forward vs trailing P/E
checks.rule: Forward P/E below trailing means earnings are expected to grow

checks.name: PEG ratio
checks.rule: Under 1.5 = growth reasonably priced; above 2.5 = paying up

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

reports.verdict: WORTH RESEARCHING — resolve the warnings first

checks.name: P/E ratio
checks.rule: Under 25 is comfortable; 25–40 needs growth to justify; above 40 is expensive
checks.note: Compare with sector peers — a 'high' P/E for a utility can be normal for software.

checks.name: Forward vs trailing P/E
checks.rule: Forward P/E below trailing means earnings are expected to grow

checks.name: PEG ratio
checks.rule: Under 1.5 = growth reasonably priced; above 2.5 = paying up

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

checks.name: P/E ratio
checks.rule: Under 25 is comfortable; 25–40 needs growth to justify; above 40 is expensive

checks.name: Forward vs trailing P/E
checks.rule: Forward P/E below trailing means earnings are expected to grow

checks.name: PEG ratio

checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk

checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5

checks.rule: Under 25 is comfortable; 25–40 needs growth to justify; above 40 is expensive
checks.note: Compare with sector peers — a 'high' P/E for a utility can be normal for software.

checks.name: Forward vs trailing P/E
checks.rule: Forward P/E below trailing

checks.name: PEG ratio
checks.rule: Under 1.5 = growth reasonably priced; above 2.5 = paying up

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5

checks.note: Compare with sector peers — a 'high' P/E for a utility can be normal for software.

checks.name: Forward vs trailing P/E
checks.rule: Forward P/E below trailing means earnings are expected to grow

checks.name: PEG ratio
checks.rule: Under 1.5 = growth reasonably priced; above 2.5 = paying up

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5

reports.verdict: WORTH RESEARCHING — resolve the warnings first

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak
checks.note: ROE above 40% alongside heavy debt or a tiny equity base is usually buyback/leverage-inflated — verify with ROIC before trusting it.

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

reports.verdict: STRONG CANDIDATE — proceed to deep research

checks.name: Earnings growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.

checks.name: Current ratio
checks.rule: Above 1.2 = can cover short-term liabilities

checks.name: Net cash position
checks.rule: Cash exceeding total debt removes refinancing risk

checks.name: Free cash flow
checks.rule: Positive FCF — the business funds itself

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% leaves room to reinvest and protect the dividend

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3 = market-like swings; higher beta needs a stronger conviction

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

reports.verdict: WORTH RESEARCHING — resolve the warnings first

checks.name: Earnings quality (OCF / net income)
checks.rule: Above 0.8 means profits are backed by real cash, not accruals

checks.name: Dividend payout ratio
checks.rule: Under 60% sustainable

checks.name: Market cap
checks.rule: Above ~2B (large/mid cap) — small caps carry extra liquidity and governance risk
checks.note: Threshold in the stock's own currency.

checks.name: Average daily volume
checks.rule: Above 100k shares/day — you can exit without moving the price

checks.name: Price vs 200-day average
checks.rule: Trading above the 200DMA = long-term uptrend intact

checks.name: Trend structure (50 vs 200 DMA)
checks.rule: 50DMA above 200DMA (golden-cross structure) supports momentum

checks.name: 52-week range position
checks.rule: 30–90% is the sweet spot; near the low may be a falling knife, at the very top is chase risk

checks.name: RSI (14-day)
checks.rule: 40–70 healthy; above 70 overbought, below 30 oversold

checks.name: Beta (volatility vs market)
checks.rule: Under 1.3

checks.name: Institutional holding
checks.rule: Above 20% means professional money has done diligence here

checks.name: Short interest
checks.rule: Under 5% of float

checks.name: Analyst consensus
checks.rule: Under 2.5 = street leans buy — a sanity check, never the reason to buy

reports.verdict: WORTH RESEARCHING — resolve the warnings first

checks.name: P/E ratio
checks.rule: Under 25 is comfortable; 25–40 needs growth to justify; above 40 is expensive
checks.note: Compare with sector peers — a 'high' P/E for a utility can be normal for software.

checks.name: Forward vs trailing P/E
checks.rule: Forward P/E below trailing means earnings are expected to grow

checks.name: PEG ratio
checks.rule: Under 1.5 = growth reasonably priced; above 2.5 = paying up

checks.name: Price to book
checks.rule: Under 3 conservative, above 8 rich (less meaningful for asset-light firms)

checks.name: EV / EBITDA
checks.rule: Under 12 comfortable; above 20 expensive

checks.name: Return on equity
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 8% weak

checks.name: Return on assets
checks.rule: Above 7% strong; below 3% weak

checks.name: Operating margin
checks.rule: Above 15% strong; below 5% thin

checks.name: Net margin
checks.rule: Positive is the floor; above 10% healthy

checks.name: Revenue growth (yoy)
checks.rule: Above 10% good; shrinking revenue is a red flag

checks.rule: Above 10% good; negative growth needs a story

checks.name: Debt to equity
checks.rule: Under 80% comfortable; above 200% leveraged
checks.note: Banks/NBFCs and utilities naturally run higher leverage — judge vs sector.
